values: The forge is cold and the smith cannot work without fuel and stock
Tool repairs have been deferred so long that people are using broken equipment
An order for nails and fittings is overdue and the builder is furious
The armorer needs refined materials and the smelter has nothing to process
We are behind on every metalwork order because the supply chain broke
The guard's equipment is in poor shape and the smith needs materials to fix it
A shipment of finished goods was promised and we have no raw stock to produce them
The locksmith needs blanks and the blacksmith needs ingots and neither has what they need
The last batch of fuel was wet and ruined an entire day's production
Without proper fasteners the new construction cannot proceed
The crafting benches are idle and the apprentices have nothing to learn on
We trade finished metalwork for food. No metal means no trade means no food
The hinges on the main gate are failing and the replacements require materials we lack
Every blade and buckle in the settlement passes through the forge eventually. The forge needs stock
The smith does not complain often. When the smith complains, I listen
